strengths: Clear identification of three specific realâworld objects with distinct contexts
Wellâexplained and coherent links between each object and the IA prompt
Strong justification for each object supported by concrete evidence, data and personal experience
Effective use of citations and authoritative references to underpin arguments
weaknesses: Analysis is at times repetitive and could benefit from greater synthesis across objects
Minor lapses in precision and occasional overâgeneralisation in explanations
Integration of TOK terminology and explicit knowledge questions is limited
Theoretical links could be made more explicit earlier in the commentary
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition meets the 7â8 band descriptors by clearly identifying three distinct realâworld objects and articulating how each illustrates the challenges of attaining certainty. Contexts are authentic and specific, links to the prompt are consistently developed, and justifications draw on relevant data, personal engagement and authoritative sources. Minor issuesâsuch as occasional repetition, slight overâgeneralisation and limited TOK terminologyâprevent a higher band placement. Overall, the work demonstrates strong evidenceâbased reasoning and coherence, warranting a final score of 7.

annotations.comment: Works Cited has relevant sources; ensure consistency in citation style (e.g., use MLA or APA uniformly).
Object 2âs depiction is well chosen and clearly labeled, situating it in an educational context and demonstrating specific real-world relevance.
When describing Darwinâs poster, incorporate discussion of paradigm shifts and falsifiability to emphasize the provisional nature of scientific knowledge.
Your reflection on accepting the theory as truth is candid. To enhance, contrast this with an example of a theory that has survived rigorous challenge to explore degrees of certainty.
The paragraph ends abruptly (âdoes not support this knowledge being regarded asâ¦â). Revise to complete your argument and eliminate this gap.
The introduction clearly defines âcertainty,â but integrating TOK definitions or sources (e.g., Knowledge claims, knowledge questions) would strengthen alignment with the IA prompt.
Choosing the Cirrus SR20 take-off table as Object 1 is perceptive; you effectively situate it in a flight-training context and show how theory informs practice.
The link from the table to the IA prompt is present but could be made more explicit earlier. Summarize how theoretical data relate to âcertainty attainable.â
Your mention of a âtheoretical, truth-based, and model-based approachâ is concise; consider expanding each approach with TOK terminology (e.g., Ways of knowing).
Your real-world context for Object 1 is vivid, demonstrating personal engagement. This personal angle reinforces the authenticity of your chosen object.
The discussion of theoretical outcomes vs actual outcomes via a bell curve is insightful. To deepen analysis, link this to specific Ways of knowing (e.g., reason, sense perception).
Consider explicitly naming George Box when discussing model fallibility, and include a citation to strengthen the authority of your claim.
BYJUâS is a popular resource but not academic; consider replacing with a primary source (e.g., Darwinâs original work or peer-reviewed article).
The NOAA graphic link cites â5 Oct. 2025â but the hurricane occurred in 2024; correct the date to ensure accuracy.
Throughout, incorporating TOK terms such as âknowledge framework,â âknowledge question,â and âcertainty vs. probabilityâ will sharpen the academic rigor.
Using phrenology as a counterexample is effective historically; explicitly connect this to the IA prompt by naming the Knowledge question it addresses.
Object 3 is clearly identified with specific metadata (date, time, source). This makes the context authentic and strengthens relevance.
The description of the hurricane cone is informative; add a link to the Knowledge framework by naming the area of knowledge (natural sciences) and its methods.
Your real-world tie to living in the cone of uncertainty is compelling, directly anchoring the objectâs significance to your personal knowledge.
The integration of George Boxâs dictum (âall models are wrongâ) is well chosen, but explain the 2/3 confidence interval in TOK terms (probability vs certainty).
Consider comparing the hurricane modelâs uncertainty with the theoretical model of Object 1 to synthesize your analysis across objects.
It would be beneficial to add a brief concluding section summarizing how all three objects together illustrate the limits of certainty.
The Cirrus SR20 handbook citation omits publisher details and uses inconsistent abbreviations; revise to include full publication information.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
